title: Problem 2 — The Incredibles ID System: toString, equals, & Static Methods
description:
Override key Object methods and write a static utility method.

You are given a `Superhero` class skeleton with three private fields already
declared. Complete the class by implementing the items below.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Fields (already declared for you):
  • private String name
  • private int strengthLevel
  • private String power
──────────────────────────────────────────────

1. CONSTRUCTOR
   Superhero(String name, String power, int strengthLevel)
   — Initializes all three fields.

2. GETTERS
   • String getName()
   • String getPower()
   • int getStrengthLevel()

3. toString()   — Override!
   Return exactly this format (mind the spaces):
     "<name> | Power: <power> | Strength: <strengthLevel>"
   Example:
     "Mr. Incredible | Power: Super strength | Strength: 95"

4. equals(Object obj)   — Override!
   Two Superheros are equal if they have the same `name` AND `power`.
   (Strength level is intentionally ignored — it may fluctuate.)
   Follow the standard pattern:
     • If obj is null or not a Superhero, return false.
     • Cast, then compare name and power.

5. static Superhero findStrongest(Superhero[] heroes)
   Loops through the array and returns the Superhero with the
   highest strengthLevel. (If the array is empty, return null.)

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• Remember: @Override is optional but good practice.
• In equals(), use .equals() for Strings, not ==.
• findStrongest is a static method on the Superhero class itself.
